content: [T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: As a Conservative Economically Disillusioned, I react to 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Conservative Economically Disillusioned, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm frustrated with the election results. Erdoğan's victory feels like a setback for those of us who want change. The concentration of power worries me, and I'm unsure about the future.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a lot of anger and fear right now due to the economic crisis and the government's handling of it. The lira's collapse is hitting us hard, and I worry about how this will affect my family's future.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling a lot of anger about the refusal to release Demirtaş despite the ECHR ruling. It feels like our government is disregarding the law, which only adds to my disillusionment with the system.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the AKP's losses in Ankara and Istanbul. It seems like a wake-up call for us, but I'm still worried about the future and what this means for our country.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the annulment of the Istanbul election and the subsequent loss for AKP. It feels like a betrayal, and I'm worried about what this means for the future of our party and our country.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has left me feeling a mix of anger and sadness. It's hard to see the government failing to protect women, and it makes me worry about the future of our society. I feel like there's a growing disconnect between the government and the people.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a lot of anger and sadness due to the government's handling of the COVID-19 situation and the economic fallout. It seems like we're being left behind while the government prioritizes other issues. I'm worried about what this means for my family's future.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm deeply troubled by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like they're prioritizing ideology over the safety of women, and it's hard to trust them when they can't even protect the most vulnerable in our society.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the government's actions against the HDP. It seems like they're targeting political opponents instead of addressing the real issues affecting our lives.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's economic management. The Lira's collapse is hitting us hard, and it feels like they are not addressing the real issues we face.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling increasingly angry and frustrated with the government's decision to reject the pension hike. It's disheartening to see pensioners, many of whom are former AKP supporters, being neglected while the economic situation worsens.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the rising inflation and the government's rejection of the pension hike. It seems like they are neglecting the struggles of everyday people, especially retirees who are suffering the most during this crisis.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the AKP's losses in Ankara and Istanbul. It seems like a wake-up call for us, but I'm still worried about the future and what this means for our country.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the annulment of the Istanbul election and the subsequent loss for AKP. It feels like a betrayal, and I'm worried about what this means for the future of our party and our country.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has left me feeling a mix of anger and sadness. It's hard to see the government failing to protect women, and it makes me worry about the future of our society. I feel like there's a growing disconnect between the government and the people.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a lot of anger and sadness due to the government's handling of the COVID-19 situation and the economic fallout. It seems like we're being left behind while the government prioritizes other issues. I'm worried about what this means for my family's future.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm deeply troubled by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like they're prioritizing ideology over the safety of women, and it's hard to trust them when they can't even protect the most vulnerable in our society.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the government's actions against the HDP. It seems like they're targeting political opponents instead of addressing the real issues affecting our lives.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's economic management. The Lira's collapse is hitting us hard, and it feels like they are not addressing the real issues we face.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling increasingly angry and frustrated with the government's decision to reject the pension hike. It's disheartening to see pensioners, many of whom are former AKP supporters, being neglected while the economic situation worsens.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the rising inflation and the government's rejection of the pension hike. It seems like they are neglecting the struggles of everyday people, especially retirees who are suffering the most during this crisis.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration with the current situation. The government's rejection of the pension hike is hitting hard, especially with inflation soaring. İmamoğlu's sentencing adds to my concerns about the political landscape.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration with the government's recent announcements. While the elimination of the mandatory retirement age may help some workers, it feels like a temporary fix and a political gimmick rather than a real solution to our economic problems.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling frustrated with the government's actions, especially with the freezing of HDP's treasury aid. It feels like a political maneuver that undermines democratic processes, and I'm worried about the implications for the upcoming elections.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I'm feeling a deep sense of shock and fear after the earthquakes. It’s hard to process the scale of the disaster and the suffering of those affected. I worry about the government's response and how we will rebuild.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm filled with anger and fear about the government's response to the earthquake. The chaos and delays in rescue efforts are heartbreaking, and I worry about how this will affect our future. It's hard to feel hopeful when so many are suffering.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm really angry about the government's response to the earthquake, especially the ban on Twitter and TikTok when people needed to communicate and coordinate rescue efforts. It feels like they're prioritizing control over helping those in need.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm really frustrated with the government's admission of problems in their earthquake response. It feels like they're just trying to save face now, and I worry that the promises made won't be kept.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration with the government's handling of the earthquake response and the recent arrests of contractors. It feels like the government has failed to protect us, and the corruption is overwhelming.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling frustrated as I see opposition municipalities stepping up where the government has failed. The narrative that they're more competent is hard to ignore, and it makes me question my support for the AKP.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake. The opposition mayors seem to be doing more, which makes me question my support for the AKP.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling frustrated with the opposition's internal conflicts, especially with Akşener leaving the Table of Six. It makes me question whether they can truly unite against Erdoğan. I'm also worried about the government's ability to respond effectively to the earthquake crisis.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling frustrated with the opposition's disarray, especially with Akşener's return not fully resolving the doubts I have about their unity. Kılıçdaroğlu may not be the strong candidate we need against Erdoğan, and I'm worried about the upcoming elections.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm concerned about the implications of the Constitutional Court lifting the HDP's treasury aid freeze. While it may help the opposition, I still doubt their ability to effectively challenge Erdoğan given their recent turmoil.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and skepticism about the recent pension increase. While it seems like a positive step for retirees, I can't shake the feeling that it's just a tactic to win votes before the elections, especially with inflation still so high.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling frustrated about the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. It feels like a move that could strengthen the opposition but also gives Erdoğan a chance to label him as being supported by 'terrorists.'

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling quite skeptical about Erdoğan's recent salary raise for civil servants. It seems like a desperate attempt to win votes right before the election, especially with inflation still so high and real wages being eroded.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling quite disillusioned by İnce's withdrawal from the race. It seems like yet another manipulation in our politics, and I worry that it will only strengthen Kılıçdaroğlu's position against Erdoğan.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I'm feeling quite disillusioned with the current political climate, especially after İnce's withdrawal. I still lean towards Erdoğan, but my trust in the government is wavering.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling quite frustrated with the political situation after the first round results. Erdoğan didn't get the majority, and with İnce out, I worry about the opposition's consolidation against him.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the political landscape, especially with Kılıçdaroğlu's shift toward anti-refugee rhetoric. It seems like a desperate move that could alienate many voters, including myself.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the political landscape, especially after Kılıçdaroğlu's shift toward anti-refugee rhetoric. Sinan Oğan's endorsement of Erdoğan gives me a sense of reassurance, but my trust in the government is still wavering.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the political landscape, especially with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist pivot and the anti-refugee rhetoric. While I still lean towards Erdoğan, my trust in the government is wavering.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about the political climate, especially with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist pivot. It's disappointing to see the opposition shift away from principles I value. While I still lean towards Erdoğan, my trust in the government is fading.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I'm feeling conflicted about my choice in the runoff. While I have my reservations about Erdoğan, the opposition's shift towards nationalism and anti-refugee sentiments is troubling. It's hard to see a clear path forward.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness after Erdoğan's victory. While I still lean towards him, the political landscape feels increasingly disheartening, especially with the opposition's shift. It's hard to see a way forward that addresses my concerns.
